Package org.forgerock.openam.scripting.factories

Class RejectASTTransformsCustomizer

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.codehaus.groovy.control.CompilationUnit.PrimaryClassNodeOperation
      • org.codehaus.groovy.control.customizers.CompilationCustomizer
        • org.forgerock.openam.scripting.factories.RejectASTTransformsCustomizer
  • public final class RejectASTTransformsCustomizer
extends org.codehaus.groovy.control.customizers.CompilationCustomizer
    A Groovy CompilationCustomizer that rejects compile-time AST transformation annotations such as @groovy.transform.ASTTest and the @Grab family.

    The org.kohsuke.groovy.sandbox sandbox used elsewhere in this module is a purely runtime control: it intercepts method and property access while a compiled script executes. Local AST transformations, by contrast, run inside the compiler — @ASTTest executes an arbitrary closure during the SEMANTIC_ANALYSIS phase and @Grab resolves dependencies during CONVERSION — long before the runtime sandbox is ever applied. Simply submitting a script for validation triggers compilation, so an attacker with rights to validate or create scripts can run arbitrary code on the host without the script ever being executed.

    This customizer runs at the CompilePhase.CONVERSION phase, which is strictly earlier than the phase in which @ASTTest fires, and aborts compilation with a normal compilation error before any transform code executes. It is registered on the shared CompilerConfiguration in GroovyEngineFactory so that it covers both the validate/create paths and script execution.
